assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run Python and pip locally.
No Pinecone account, API key, index, or sample records exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a Pinecone account, obtain an API key, and complete the official Quickstart through a semantic search that returns ranked hits.

first_success.outcome: Sample records are upserted and a text query returns searchable hits from the quickstart index.
first_success.observable_signal: The Quickstart search loop prints hit scores and content fields for the sample query.

route_selection.surface: Pinecone console signup plus official Database Quickstart Python SDK path
route_selection.rule: Prefer a free Starter plan when it reaches the same Quickstart outcome; do not invent a winner among Starter, Builder, and Standard trial when plan choice changes cost and region limits.

candidates.name: Starter plan (free) then Python SDK Quickstart
candidates.reason: Docs present Starter as free with one-region limits, but signup fields and whether Starter can run the documented aws/us-east-1 create_index_for_model path are not fully verified from public evidence alone.

candidates.name: Builder plan or Standard plan trial then Python SDK Quickstart
candidates.reason: These plans change payment and entitlement relative to Starter and are consequential under the lexicographic payment rule.

candidates.name: AI coding-tool plugin Quickstart
candidates.reason: The Quickstart presents the IDE-plugin path as an alternate surface from the self-service SDK route.

required_path.action: Sign up at app.pinecone.io and choose a plan.

required_fields.label: Plan selection
required_fields.notes: Starter, Builder, and Standard trial are documented choices.

required_fields.label: Account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Public console shell does not expose a static registration field contract.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Pinecone account on a chosen plan.

required_path.action: Create or copy a Pinecone API key from the console.
required_path.observable_result: An API key value is available for the SDK client.
The pinecone package is available locally.

required_path.action: Initialize the client, create the quickstart index for the hosted embedding model if needed, upsert sample records, wait briefly, and search.

required_fields.label: Index name, cloud, region, and embed model

required_path.observable_result: Search prints ranked hits for the sample query.

prerequisites.description: Local Python and pip are available for the SDK Quickstart.

external_gates.description: Plan selection can introduce payment for Builder or trial entitlements for Standard.

unavoidable_waits.description: The Quickstart inserts a short sleep after upsert so records become searchable.

platform_outcomes.description: Pinecone creates the index for the hosted embedding model and serves search hits.

excluded.item: Reading Quickstart documentation and optional rerank elaboration pages
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.
Different interaction surface from the self-service SDK Quickstart.

excluded.item: Optional integrated rerank with bge-reranker-v2-m3 after first search hits
excluded.reason: First searchable hits already satisfy an earliest meaningful Quickstart success; rerank is an additional demonstration in the same page.

sources.title: Quickstart
evidence_supported: Starter, Builder, and Standard trial plan choices

sources.title: Pinecone Console
evidence_supported: Static registration field contract not exposed without authenticated signup

uncertainties.question: Which plan is required for the documented aws/us-east-1 create_index_for_model Quickstart, and do Starter limits block that path?
uncertainties.impact: If Starter cannot host the documented region or model path, the free route fails and a paid or trial plan becomes required.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official plan-limit confirmation or a fresh Starter-account walkthrough that either completes or blocks the Quickstart create_index_for_model call.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, OAuth options, agreements, and verification steps appear on app.pinecone.io registration?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation actions and fields cannot be counted without inventing auth UI.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Observed-public-ui screenshots or a read-only cold signup walkthrough of the current console register flow.
